trustees.name: Deborah Enilo Ajakaiye
trustees.title: Prof.
trustees.bio: Prof. Deborah Ajakaiye is the Founder and President of CCWA. She studied at the University of Ibadan and graduated with an Honours degree in Physics in 1962, holds a Masters degree in Applied Geophysics from the University of Birmingham, UK in 1964, and a Ph.D. in Applied Geophysics from Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria, Nigeria in 1970. She has held various posts internationally and within Nigeria, including Chairman/President (Co-founder) of the Association of Geoscientists for International Development (AGID); President of the Nigerian Mining and Geosciences Society; Vice President of the Association of Geophysicists involved in Exploration and Research in Africa (AGERA); President of the African Region of the American Association of Petroleum Geologists (AAPG); and Head of Department of Physics and Dean of Faculty of Natural Sciences at Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria, and University of Jos. She has won numerous awards both within Nigeria and internationally as a geophysicist, and derives great joy and fulfilment in replacing gloom with smiles on the faces of the less privileged — Widows, Widowers, Aged, and Orphans.

trustees.name: Francis Kehinde Kashimawo
trustees.title: Dr.
trustees.bio: Dr. Francis Kehinde Kashimawo graduated as a Medical Doctor from University College Hospital, Ibadan, Nigeria in 1986 and had a postgraduate training in Public Health at the University of Leeds, UK. He has an International Diploma in Humanitarian Assistance (IDHA) from the Geneva, Switzerland Centre for International Humanitarian Cooperation in cooperation with Fordham University, the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland, and the United Nations System Staff College. Knowledgeable in forging trans-cultural communication and relationships in a not-for-profit organization for 20 years, Francis has been coordinating health care, microcredit, rural development and provision of basic education to peoples of varying tribes and cultures in places of very difficult geographical accessibility in northern Nigeria. He was the pioneer chairperson and is now a member of the Board of Trustees of NiCAN (now CNAN), a nationwide network of careers for people living with HIV and AIDS. He is also involved in the leadership of the Christian Medical and Dental Association of Nigeria (CMDA Nigeria), and is currently the pioneer Dean of the Institute of Medical Missions, whose aim is to inculcate Christian discipline and service to humanity in medical students and younger medical colleagues.

trustees.name: Danladi Verheijen
trustees.title: Mr.
trustees.bio: Mr. Danladi Verheijen is the Chief Executive Officer of Verod Capital Limited, a Lagos-based corporate finance and investment company. Prior to Verod, he served as a Vice President at Citibank Nigeria. He has also held roles at Ocean and Oil Holdings and at McKinsey & Company. Danladi has an MBA from Harvard Business School, an MS in Engineering Economic Systems & Operations Research from Stanford University, and a BS in Electrical Engineering from Calvin College. He is also an Alumni Board member of Calvin College as well as an executive member of the Harvard Business School Associate of Nigeria (HBSAN).
